paragraphs.body: The Beauty Behind All The Chaos

In the article, “Bermuda Triangle Demystified,” Aria Dismount Nathan acknowledges all the mysteries and myths of the Bermuda Triangle, and which ones can be justified and the others that don’t have any explanation. She justifies her point by developing several important ideas. First, Robinson acknowledges the reputation of the Bermuda Triangle, long before the myth became anything popular. She describes the terrors of long-ago sea travelers that nicknamed Bermuda the “enchanted island”. The origin of all these myths date back to around the time of Columbus. She uses the book “The Tempest,” by Shakespeare and the article “The Deadly Bermuda Triangle,” by Vincent Gaddis to use as evidence. Next, she accounts for all the disappearances that have taken place. She acknowledges all the ancient tales and gives plenty of of examples talking about all the accounted for disappearances and weird discoveries. Finally, she justifies all the disappearances with scientific theories that could be responsible for the disasters. Some people claim that the disappearances have been caused by alien abductions or sorcery, however, she claims there are plenty of scientific explanations for the mysteries. She states that they are all linked back to extreme weather conditions and methane gas. Even though these can justify some of the myths, she still believes that some mysteries will never be solved, and there is a magic to it all. The Bermuda Triangle isn’t the only thing that is full of uncertainty. Our lives are. Personally, my parents’ addiction is an uncertainty. Both of my parents have battled with their own issues and struggles, but even when they may be “clean”, there is still a risk that it could happen again. Ever since I was a little girl, I would say around third grade, I was aware of addiction was. Both had past issues of struggling with substance abuse when they were teenagers, but when my parents met, they were in recovery. My parents had me young, so they were not fully equipping to take on the responsibility of a child, but they made it work. As I was growing up, I lived with my mom and visited my dad on the weekends, they were very good at co-parenting. I was always able to have two of everything and I loved it as a little girl, and even though I had two of everything, my parents still would come to each other’s family holidays and allow for family time, so I never has any worries. When I was in halfway through my sixth-grade year, I was visiting my dad during the weekend, and we received a call from my grandmother telling us my mother had been arrested and we didn’t know when she would be released. I ended up moving in with my dad and transferring schools halfway through that year, little did I know, my dad was also using when I moved in with him and my other grandmother. Fast forwarding to eighth grade my mother had been clean, and I moved back in with her and my grandmother. My father’s battle with heroin had become extremely devastating and he had put me in danger so my mother thought it would be best to stop seeing him as much, and those visits became nothing, and the phone stopped ringing. He has been in and out of my life ever since it started back in eigth-grade and is still currently happening to this day. The severity of my mother’s situation, on the other hand, begins more recently. When I was young, my mother was using throughout my childhood, but I was completely unaware of it because I was a child, and I was supposed to be protected from those kinds of issues. Ever since I had moved back in with my mom, we rebuilt our relationship and we became best friends, she was my best friend. I noticed a change between her and I around a year ago, I slowly started to distance myself away from her because of her actions, but we remained close. More recently we became more distant, and I noticed something was off but never suspected the worst, I just brushed it off. My mother decided that she would be moving out and never wanted to speak to me again or have me as her responsibility anymore. This by far completely broke me apart. We came to later find out she had begun using drugs again and it wasn’t just alcohol. I was devastated hearing this news, and she still has no idea that I know to this day. At that point and still to this day I must ask myself what was real and what was just all just a lie. I will never know why. I grew up in destruction and the chaos of life and will never know why it had to happen to me.

paragraphs.body: I have learned throughout the years that I must put myself first. To some this may seem selfish, but to me it’s to survive. Do what you have to do. If you continue to allow someone back into your life that apologizes but refuses to change, its manipulation. I have realized that I tend to judge people a lot quicker than I should because of my protective instinct I have due to the destruction my parents have caused in my life. It is okay to feel different emotions that others may not, such as hating your own parents. Someone who has both parents in their life with good relationships couldn’t imagine hating their parents. However, we can, and that’s okay. I personally still struggle with the word hate, when there are so many bad memories, it is hard to remember the good. Because there was a time that both of my parents where amazing people, but they never grew up. So yes, I still love them, but I also hate them for what they did to me, the innocence that has been taken away from me because of their mistakes, and that’s okay. There will always be an uncertainly because if it happens once, it can always happen again. Disaster can happen at any given moment and there may be no signs of it coming. You never know what may be destroyed within a matter of seconds. Addiction is a disease that not only affects the addict, but it drowns everyone that loves them as well. In the article, Bermuda Triangle Demystified, Nathan indicates, “But perhaps the most damning tales were told by sailors terrified of shipwreck on Bermuda's treacherous stretch of reefs.” This quote makes an inference that all the myths originated from those who have experienced such horrors of the Triangle and have had to be cautious of getting caught up in the storm. This relates back to addiction because the only people that know the true outcomes of what happens with addicts are the people that have experienced to firsthand and watched it happen. They themselves are scared that one day that will be them or one day they are never going to live a peaceful life. I am here to tell you that no matter what disasters happen, and you feel like it will never get better, there will always be a beauty to it all. So yes, there are explanations to everything, and reality is true, but there is something special that will always be there. Towards the end of the article, Robinson closes with the statement, “But don't let the legend scare you away -- the Triangle isn't the only thing that makes this island seem magical.” This gives hope that there is still beauty and magic to the island, despite all the rumors and reputation. She uses this quote to shed light on all the darkness and bring some hope to it.

paragraphs.body: I have overcome my experiences by surround myself with people that I love, focusing on my schooling, and appreciating the little things in life. I still struggle with my circumstances at time, but I have overcome what I thought was the end of my world. We can’t sit and dwell on what could have been. We must push ourselves to be better than what we have experience. We must make a beautiful life for ourselves regardless of if our parents chose drugs over their own child. It may be hard to realize at times, but everything will be okay. As author Aria Nathan implied, there is still a little magic to it all, so we must find that magic, and not let go.

annotations.text: As author Aria Nathan implied, there is still a little magic to it all, so we must find that magic, and not let go.
annotations.comment: lovely line. Again, though, it feels like you still need to develop the "magic," the "beauty," to make this essay feel completely whole.
